cashback.notes: Shares a combined ₹500/month cap with the Swiggy, Zomato, Ola category
Shares the combined ₹500/month cap with Google Pay bill payments
Uncapped; rent, fuel, wallet loads, EMI, gold/jewellery, cash advances and insurance are excluded from cashback

loungeAccess.conditions: Up to 4 domestic lounge visits a year, gated on ₹50,000 spend in the preceding 3 months; waived for the first 4 months on a new card.

source.name: CardInsider
source.document: All Major Axis Bank Credit Cards Devalued – Effective April 2024

faqTemplateAnswers.Is this card lifetime free?: No, it has a ₹499 joining fee and ₹499 annual fee (from the second year), which Axis waives if you spend ₹2,00,000 or more in the preceding year.
faqTemplateAnswers.Is it worth it?: Yes, for most everyday spenders — 5% on Google Pay bill payments and 4% on Swiggy/Zomato/Ola, plus an uncapped 1.5% base rate, comfortably outweigh the low ₹499 fee, though the shared ₹500/month cap limits value for very high spenders in those categories.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should get this card?: People who pay recurring bills or recharges through Google Pay and regularly order via Swiggy, Zomato, or book Ola cabs, and want a low-fee everyday cashback card.
faqTemplateAnswers.Does it support UPI?: The standard Visa ACE card is not UPI-linkable, but Axis also issues an ACE RuPay variant that can be linked to UPI apps.
faqTemplateAnswers.Can I get lounge access with this card?: Yes, up to 4 complimentary domestic lounge visits a year, but only if you spent ₹50,000 or more in the preceding 3 months (waived for the first 4 months after issuance).
faqTemplateAnswers.What income is required to qualify?: Axis Bank does not publish a fixed minimum income for the ACE card; applicants generally need a steady monthly income, an existing banking relationship, and a reasonable credit score.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should avoid this card?: Users who don't pay bills via Google Pay or use Swiggy/Zomato/Ola won't get much value beyond the flat 1.5%, and heavy spenders in the bonus categories will find the ₹500/month combined cap limiting.
faqTemplateAnswers.What are the best alternatives?: Flipkart Axis Bank Credit Card if you shop heavily online, or Amazon Pay ICICI Credit Card for a lifetime-free alternative with strong Amazon cashback.
